description_long: This is a rectangular, neoclassical train station building with a flat, ornate roof and a symmetrical facade. The front features three large arched doorways with decorative columns and pediments, flanked by pilasters and detailed stonework. The exterior walls are constructed from weathered, light gray stone, with visible patches of moss and signs of age, giving it an abandoned or historic appearance. The side and rear facades have two levels of arched windows, evenly spaced, with the upper level being smaller and the lower level larger. The roof is bordered by a repeating pattern of raised stonework, adding to the classical architectural feel. The structure sits on a circular base that resembles a dirt or gravel plaza, suggesting it is set apart from surrounding buildings.
description: A weathered, neoclassical stone train station with arched doorways, ornate roof detailing, and mossy, aged walls. The building features symmetrical facades and sits on a circular dirt base.
description_view_0: Front view showing three large arched doorways, decorative columns, and detailed stonework above the entrances.
description_view_1: Side view displaying two levels of arched windows, with visible moss and no main entrances.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with less ornate detailing and no large doorways.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two levels of arched windows and weathered stone, mirroring view 1.
